run_metadata.coverage_summary: DEGRADED RUN (headline / search-snippet level) for the THIRD consecutive day. WebFetch remains fully blocked by this session's outbound egress policy: every WebFetch call returned HTTP 403, confirmed against a control host (example.com returned 403 identically to tenders.gov.au), so this is a blanket organisation egress-policy denial recorded at the proxy gateway, not per-site bot protection. Per /root/.ccr/README.md a proxy 403 must be reported rather than retried or bypassed. WebSearch (server-side, not routed through the egress proxy) was the only functioning web tool and was used exclusively. Consequently NO tender detail page or specification document could be opened this run: every field below was taken from WebSearch result snippets that quote the AusTender / portal listing rows (identity, buyer, publish/closing dates, ATM ID, and — where the index surfaced it — the notice's own description text). No documents, evaluation criteria, contacts, or values could be verified against an opened notice, so all 7 tenders are counted in entries_pending_detail. Tier-1 Commonwealth (AusTender) was productive: 7 confirmable current ATMs, of which 2 are genuinely newly-seen this run (S-EST10741 HMAS Stirling System Works; S-EST11096 Lavarack Barracks Irrigation Upgrades) and 5 are still-open opportunities carried from earlier runs (re-reported to advance last_seen/active status). Tier-1 state/territory portals (NSW buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders, Tenders WA/SA/TAS, NT QTOL, Tenders ACT) returned only landing pages via the search index; one confirmable open VIC item (DOT43290) is carried. Source discovery (mission half 2) was productive: 6 genuinely new buyer-originated Australian sources were identified via WebSearch and recorded in discovered_sources, plus a self-heal of the QTenders host (hpw.qld.gov.au -> epw.qld.gov.au). Tier-3 corporate / GBE opportunity detail remained login/invitation-gated as expected; no corporate RFx was fabricated and none was publicly visible via the search index this run.
gaps: CRITICAL (3rd consecutive day): WebFetch is denied by the session outbound egress policy for ALL hosts. Every WebFetch call returned HTTP 403, confirmed against the control host example.com (403) and against tenders.gov.au (403). The proxy status endpoint reported enabled=true with no recentRelayFailures, and /root/.ccr/README.md classifies a proxy 403 as an organisation egress-policy denial that must be reported and NOT retried or routed around. WebSearch was the only functioning web tool this session. Runs on 2026-07-03 and 2026-07-04 hit the identical block; runs on/before 2026-07-02 clearly had WebFetch access, so this is a persistent change in this session's egress allowlist, not a transient outage. ACTION NEEDED: allow outbound HTTPS to the tender portals so detail/deliverables extraction can resume.

No tender detail page or specification document could be opened this run. For every tender captured, requirements, eligibility, evaluation_criteria, documents, contacts and (except where the AusTender listing snippet stated it) value/contract_term could not be verified against the notice and are null/empty. Where a deliverables_summary or scope is populated it was taken verbatim from the AusTender / portal listing text surfaced in a WebSearch result snippet, NOT from an opened detail page or tender document; treat as high-but-not-page-verified confidence. All 7 tenders are counted in entries_pending_detail and need a documents-level detail pass once fetch access is restored.

AusTender ATM S-EST10741 (HMAS Stirling System Works, Defence DSRG, WA) was confirmed as a current ATM with a works description via the search index, but its closing_date, publish_time and full agency line could not be captured from the snippet; closing_date is left null pending a detail pass.

Tier-1 state/territory portals — NSW buy.nsw/eTendering, QTenders (QLD, now qtenders.epw.qld.gov.au), Tenders WA, Tenders SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, Tenders ACT, Buying for Victoria — were unreachable via WebFetch (403). The search index returned only generic portal landing pages plus aggregator/TenderLink rows; apart from the carried Buying for Victoria item DOT43290, no fresh non-excluded STATE government tender detail could be captured with confidence this run.

Tier-2 (councils/GBEs/universities/health) and Tier-3 (corporate): VendorPanel public tenders and every individual buyer portal (HealthShare NSW, Queensland Health, SAP Ariba Discovery, BHP, Rio Tinto, etc.) were unreachable via WebFetch (403). Genuine buyer-originated portal URLs were recovered from the search index and recorded in discovered_sources, but no individual opportunity could be opened or verified, and no corporate/GBE RFx was recorded as a tender.

Tier-3 corporate e-sourcing hubs (SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery, Coupa, Jaggaer, RangeMe) and major-buyer portals could not be opened; per prior fetch-verified runs and search evidence their opportunity detail is login- or invitation-gated. No publicly-visible corporate RFx could be confirmed this run, so none was recorded as a tender (portals remain captured in discovered_sources with access_method=portal-registration). Invitation-only corporate RFPs (e.g. management-consultancy panels) remain out of scope and unobservable.
AusTender ATM_2026_6551 recurred across several searches but no confirmable title/agency/closing snippet could be captured; deferred to a future run.

URL self-heal: the QTenders (Queensland) live host is now qtenders.epw.qld.gov.au; the seed list and prior discovered_sources used qtenders.hpw.qld.gov.au. Recorded corrected URL in discovered_sources.
WebSearch is US-geolocated, which limits freshness/geo-ranking of Australian results and may miss items published in the last 24 hours that have not yet been indexed.
recommended_seed_additions: Transport for NSW — Tenders and contracts (transport.nsw.gov.au/operations/roads-and-waterways/business-and-industry/partners-and-suppliers/tenders-and-contracts) — major state transport buyer publishing via NSW eTendering; distinct from the general buy.nsw feed

HealthShare NSW — Procurement & Supply Chain (healthshare.nsw.gov.au/services/procurement) — NSW Health central goods/services procurement (~100 statewide contracts); distinct from the NSW Health tenders landing page already known

Sutherland Shire Council — Current tenders (sutherlandshire.nsw.gov.au/local-business/doing-business-with-council/tender-opportunities/current-tenders) — local-government buyer current-tenders register
Central Coast Council — Tenders (centralcoast.nsw.gov.au/business/tenders) — local-government buyer tender page (NSW)
Townsville Regional Council — Current tenders (tr.qld.gov.au/community-business/business-support/tenders-contracts) — local-government buyer (QLD)
WALGA Preferred Supplier Program (walga.asn.au/suppliers/preferred-supplier-program/become-a-preferred-supplier) — WA Local Government Association preferred-supplier panels used by member councils
QTenders (Queensland) — URL correction only: live host is now qtenders.epw.qld.gov.au (previously listed as qtenders.hpw.qld.gov.au)

tenders.title: HMAS Stirling System Works
tenders.category: Building construction and support and maintenance and repair services
tenders.description: Request for tender for system works at HMAS Stirling, Western Australia, comprising investigations, demolition, system replacement, and the supply and installation of new fencing and gates.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The supplier is to deliver building/system works at HMAS Stirling including investigations, demolition of existing elements, system replacement, and supply and installation of new fencing and gates. Full scope could not be confirmed from an opened notice this run.
key_deliverables: Investigations
Demolition of existing elements
System replacement
Supply and install new fencing and gates
tenders.reasoning: Discovered on AusTender (Tier-1 Commonwealth, buyer-originated) via WebSearch of current ATMs. Confirmed as a current ATM published 2-Jul-2026; the works description was surfaced in the search result snippet quoting the AusTender listing.
tenders.notes: HEADLINE / SNIPPET LEVEL ONLY — WebFetch blocked (403) this run, so the detail page could not be opened. closing_date, publish_time, exact agency line, requirements, evaluation criteria, documents, contacts and value are unverified/null pending a detail pass. Genuinely newly-seen this run.

tenders.title: Lavarack Barracks Irrigation Upgrades, QLD
tenders.category: Building construction and support and maintenance and repair services
tenders.description: Request for tender to upgrade irrigation at Lavarack Barracks, Townsville QLD, involving demolition and decommissioning of existing irrigation equipment, earthworks, a new irrigation system and associated multidisciplinary works.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The supplier is to deliver irrigation upgrade works at Lavarack Barracks: demolition and decommissioning of existing irrigation equipment, earthworks, a concrete pad, a new irrigation system and pipework, together with civil, architectural, hydraulic, landscaping and electrical works.
key_deliverables: Demolition and decommissioning of existing irrigation equipment
Earthworks and concrete pad
New irrigation system and pipework
Civil, architectural, hydraulic works
Landscaping and electrical works
tenders.reasoning: Discovered on AusTender (Tier-1 Commonwealth, buyer-originated) via WebSearch of current ATMs. Title, agency, category (UNSPSC 72100000), close date 14-Jul-2026 12:00pm ACT, publish date 10-Jun-2026 and works description were all surfaced in the search result snippet quoting the AusTender listing.
tenders.notes: HEADLINE / SNIPPET LEVEL — WebFetch blocked (403) so the detail page/documents could not be opened; requirements, evaluation criteria, documents, contacts and value are unverified/null pending a detail pass. Published 10-Jun-2026 but first observed by this routine this run (missed by earlier runs); still open (closes 14-Jul-2026).

tenders.title: GEMS verification testing — accredited testing laboratory services (panel)
tenders.buyer: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water
tenders.description: Request for tender (panel arrangement) for accredited testing laboratories to undertake verification testing of products regulated under the Greenhouse and Energy Minimum Standards Act 2012 (GEMS Act), to confirm compliance with mandatory regulatory requirements.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The Department requires the services of testing laboratories with appropriate accreditation to undertake verification testing of products regulated under the GEMS Act 2012. This testing verifies compliance with mandatory regulatory requirements and confirms the accuracy of energy-efficiency and performance claims made by manufacturers and suppliers. A panel deed is anticipated to commence 1 November 2026 and conclude 30 June 2030.
key_deliverables: Verification testing of GEMS-regulated products
Confirmation of compliance with mandatory GEMS regulatory requirements
Verification of energy-efficiency and performance claims
requirements: Appropriate laboratory accreditation to undertake the verification testing
tenders.eligibility: Open to all; panel arrangement (multiple providers anticipated)
mandatory_conditions: Testing laboratory accreditation appropriate to GEMS verification testing
tenders.delivery_location: ACT, NSW, VIC, SA, WA, QLD, NT, TAS and overseas
tenders.reasoning: Tier-1 Commonwealth (AusTender), buyer-originated. Re-reported to advance last_seen/active status; still open (closes 12-Aug-2026). Scope, panel term and delivery locations were surfaced in the search result snippet quoting the AusTender listing.
tenders.notes: Carried from 2026-07-03. HEADLINE / SNIPPET LEVEL — WebFetch blocked (403) so the detail page/documents could not be opened; evaluation criteria, documents, contacts and value remain unverified/null pending a detail pass.

tenders.title: Wharf 7 and South Wharf remediation works
tenders.category: Building construction and support and maintenance and repair services
tenders.description: Request for tender to appoint a qualified organisation to remediate the wharf structures forming part of Wharf 7 and the South Wharf, restoring the functional stability of the wharves.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The Museum seeks to appoint an appropriately qualified organisation to perform works involving the remediation of wharf structures which form part of Wharf 7 and the South Wharf, with the objective of restoring the functional stability of the wharves by replacing severely compromised construction elements and repairing deteriorated construction elements where feasible.
key_deliverables: Remediation of Wharf 7 and South Wharf structures
Replacement of severely compromised construction elements
Repair of deteriorated construction elements where feasible
Restoration of functional stability of the wharves
requirements: Appropriately qualified organisation for marine/wharf remediation works
tenders.delivery_location: Darling Harbour, Sydney, NSW (Australian National Maritime Museum)
tenders.reasoning: Tier-1 Commonwealth (AusTender), buyer-originated (Australian National Maritime Museum). Re-reported to advance last_seen/active status; still open (closes 12-Aug-2026). Scope was surfaced in the search result snippet quoting the AusTender listing.
tenders.notes: Carried from 2026-07-03. HEADLINE / SNIPPET LEVEL — WebFetch blocked (403) so the detail page/documents could not be opened; evaluation criteria, documents, contacts and value remain unverified/null pending a detail pass.

discovered_sources.source_name: Transport for NSW — Tenders and contracts
discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for NSW transport/civil opportunities; Transport for NSW publishes its major construction and professional-services tenders on the NSW eTendering platform and lists current opportunities (e.g. TTSS Gen 3, Sydney Harbour Bridge Laterals Access, Dobroyd Parade Pedestrian Bridge) on its own tenders-and-contracts page.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated state transport authority; documents downloadable only by registered NSW eTendering users. Distinct from the general buy.nsw feed already tracked.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for NSW hospital/health procurement; HealthShare NSW is NSW Health's central point for goods and services tendering and contracting, managing ~100 statewide contracts (e.g. a 1-Jul-2026 pharmaceutical supply panel award administered by HealthShare NSW surfaced in results).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated public-health procurement body; distinct from the NSW Health tenders landing page already tracked. Individual opportunities run via the NSW Health Gateway / eTendering.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for NSW council current tenders; council publishes its own current-tenders register.
WebSearch for VendorPanel/council local-government tenders; Central Coast Council publishes its own tenders page.
WebSearch for council current tenders; Townsville Regional Council publishes its own current-tenders register.
WebSearch for VendorPanel/WA local-government supplier panels; the WA Local Government Association operates a Preferred Supplier Program (panels) used by member councils.
discovered_sources.notes: Peak-body panel arrangement covering WA member councils; buyer-originated for local-government procurement. Confidence medium as it is a panel/registration channel rather than a live open-tender feed.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: URL self-heal: WebSearch returned the live QTenders host as qtenders.epw.qld.gov.au; the seed list and prior discovered_sources used qtenders.hpw.qld.gov.au (host moved from HPW to EPW/Department of Energy and Public Works branding).
discovered_sources.notes: Not a new source — URL correction only. Recorded so the known-source list points at the live host. QLD is also migrating some procurement to VendorPanel via supply.qld.gov.au (already tracked).
